entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: Environmental change that gives rise to numerous environmental stresses including soil salinity

entities.name: SALINITY STRESS
entities.description: Soil salinity that affects agricultural productivity by damaging physiological, biochemical, and molecular processes

entities.name: AGRICULTURAL PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The output of agricultural land, which is negatively affected by salinity stress

entities.name: PLANT GROWTH
entities.description: The physical development of plants, which is negatively affected by salinity

entities.name: SALT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of plants to withstand high salt concentrations, crucial for sustaining agricultural productivity

entities.name: ION HOMEOSTASIS
entities.description: The regulation of ion concentrations within plant cells, modulated in response to salinity

entities.name: ANTIOXIDANT DEFENSE SYSTEM
entities.description: A biological system induced by salinity to protect plants from oxidative damage

entities.name: PHYTOHORMONES
entities.description: Plant hormones whose biosynthesis is modulated in response to salinity stress

entities.name: OSMOPROTECTANTS
entities.description: Molecules synthesized by plants to protect against osmotic stress and ion toxicity

entities.name: OSMOTIC STRESS
entities.description: Stress caused by water potential differences, often resulting from salinity

entities.name: ION TOXICITY
entities.description: Damage caused by the accumulation of toxic ions in plant tissues

entities.name: REACTIVE OXYGEN SPECIES SCAVENGING
entities.description: The process of removing harmful reactive oxygen species, augmented by the antioxidant defense system

entities.name: CROP PLANTS
entities.description: Plants grown for food or economic purposes that are generally sensitive to salinity

entities.name: STRESS-RELATED GENES
entities.description: Genes triggered by plants to cope with the adverse consequences of salinity

entities.name: OMICS
entities.description: High-throughput biological analysis approaches including genomics, transcriptomics, proteomics, and metabolomics

entities.name: GENOMICS
entities.description: The study of the complete set of DNA within an organism

entities.name: TRANSCRIPTOMICS
entities.description: The study of the complete set of RNA transcripts produced by the genome

entities.name: PROTEOMICS
entities.description: The large-scale study of proteins

entities.name: METABOLOMICS
entities.description: The study of small molecule metabolites

entities.name: GENOME EDITING TOOLS
entities.description: Biotechnological tools used to precisely modify the DNA of plants to improve traits

entities.name: ZFN
entities.description: Zinc Finger Nucleases used for targeted genome editing

entities.name: TALEN
entities.description: Transcription Activator-Like Effector Nucleases used for targeted genome editing

entities.name: CRISPR/CAS SYSTEM
entities.description: A modern genome editing tool based on clustered regularly interspaced short palindromic repeats

entities.name: ZERO HUNGER
entities.description: A worldwide sustainable development goal aimed at ending hunger

entities.name: FAO
entities.description: The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations
